title: Sort by for Dropdown list
excerpt:
Hi there, 
I have a huge list of suppliers, I would like to edit easily and sort my dropdown list in alphabetical order. 
Is this possible? Can we edit somewhere all our items for each type of column? 
Thank you, 
Céline…

posts.author: Celine (Céline Vignal)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
I have a huge list of suppliers, I would like to edit easily and sort my dropdown list in alphabetical order.
Is this possible? Can we edit somewhere all our items for each type of column?
Thank you,
Céline.

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve asked this same question to support and was told that this feature is not yet available. I would love a way to alphabetize and mass edit a dropdown column as my team uses it for our customers. I was able to alphabetize the list in excel and then copy and paste it into my board. However, the new dropdown entries I add will not be alphabetized.

posts.author: Celine (Céline Vignal)
posts.cookedHtml:
@cfraticelli Damned! But thank you for your time
I hope they will implement that soon.

posts.author: clayanderson (Clay Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this! In fact, could you just make the dropdown labels display alphabetically by default? The inability to order the dropdown labels is a frustrating limitation. The dropdown performs a function that can’t quite be replicated by the status column (too few options) or tag column (tags are too global and easy-to-edit).

posts.author: mbrennan (Matt Brenan)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. Very frustrating. Monday.com please add.

posts.author: Mondayfan (Mondayfan)
posts.cookedHtml:
New Monday trial user here.

For this reason alone, we may stop our entire company from switching over to Monday as we use dropdown list extensively.

I’m shocked that for a feature that is so basic is not implemented yet. Does anyone know if this will be a permanent limitation?

posts.author: nikolamujdzic (Nikola Mujdzic Rescic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also I have two suggestions and a bug I have already reported.

  1. Dropdown by its nature should be only one choice, and checkbox should be multiple choice.
  2. It should put in settings of the dropdown to sort entries of choices by alphabetical order or by entry order.

Now to a bug problem: I have created a Board with form view to collect NGO members applications. I used that board only for applying to the NGO, verifying entries, and duplicated board structure to move verified entries to Board 2 which was verified members board. I have copied the cities list and paste to a dropdown column. I have probably pasted null value with 500 cities, and something happened with cities entries. After I moved pulses to Board 2 I have lost all the cities data from the dropdown column. And after a while my board was nonresponsive to new form entries and to edit cities (dropdown column)

posts.author: kswiss50 (Kendall Shortt)
posts.cookedHtml:
@nikolamujdzic Am I understanding you correctly?

  • Dropdown should be used for one choice

  • Checkbox is multiple choice

  • Status- can only be used for one choice

This seems backward to me based on my personal workflow.

I need dropdown to be used for multiple items but also dynamic so I can add data later on. This is where sorting is key.

A way around this would be to add another board with all of those options but unfortunately, you can’t submit a form with a Link to Item column, yet so this takes this option out for me.

Statuses are very limited on colors (30 max) , and since we can’t do Link to Item, the dropdown is the only option left.

That seems to be a very difficult thing to get around for my workflow and maybe others as well.

posts.author: Andres (Andres)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be very useful to be able to rearrange the order of dropdown items, just as you rearrange the status column! We continously create new items, but do not follow the logical order they should be in.

Thanks for considering!

posts.author: KyleDanielle (Kyle Seem)
posts.cookedHtml: Just wanted to add my voice to the request to be able to reorder labels, or at the very least, have them automatically read alphabetically. This would be incredibly helpful.

posts.author: ASHEPHERD (Adam Shepherd)
posts.cookedHtml: Bump. This should be a basic feature.

posts.author: kpatped (Kirstie)
posts.cookedHtml:
We absolutely need to be able to have the drop down options presented alphabetically as a start. Being able to arrange the order would be even better. Monday.com competitors offer this - please add.

It looks amateurish in forms to not have the options in a logical order and takes time for our team to find the right options internally. A huge time waste!

posts.author: bvanover (Brandon Vanover)
posts.cookedHtml: Requesting this feature in 2021. This is important for our work request forms.

posts.author: marcbrown (Marc Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday, are you listening? Please add this feature. It looks like it started in 2019. I haven’t seen anyone from Monday’s team even respond to this thread.

posts.author: travis.mcclellan (Travis McClellan)
posts.cookedHtml: I will keep this cause going by adding to this thread. Not just for dropdown lists, but also for status columns as well. On several boards I will have a pretty big list of statuses, and it stinks to move them around manually.

posts.author: adydent23 (Adrian Dent)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree on the need to provide by default the list alphabetically, though allowing the flexibility to drag and reorder a list, as you can within the Status feature would be extra useful.

posts.author: adydent23 (Adrian Dent)
posts.cookedHtml: Checking today it appears the list ordered alphabetically is now available! Great news. Already helping.

posts.author: Gerlof (Gerlof Kasma)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t find the option to sort the dropdown list items alphabetically?

posts.author: Gerlof (Gerlof Kasma)
posts.cookedHtml: Ok, so on the items board it appears in order when selecting a country. However, when you edit the options it’s still not in alphabetical order. Also, I use a project request form, and the dropdown options there are not in order either, just in order of time created. This needs to be fixed asap, it is very annoying.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

You should be able to sort the dropdown column in alphabetical order using the sort feature, and saving the sort as a new view:

Screen Shot 2022-11-14 at 11.56.22 am804×398 25.4 KB

From there, all items should appear in the correct order based upon the alphabetical order of the dropdown values.

You also have the ability to save the sorted view as the default board view:

Screen Shot 2022-11-14 at 11.57.43 am760×384 31.4 KB

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,

Thanks for the reply on this issue, but this seems to sort the entire board vs. sorting the dropdown menu items.

For example, On my Sales Overview Board I have various accounts that I include in a connected column, I can select one or more accounts for this column, which is perfect.

My problem is the information that I input on the Accounts Board is in alphabetical order (thanks to the sort option), but when I go to my Sales Overview Board and I click the plus to select an account, the names are shown in the order I added them on the Accounts Board, not in the alphabetical order as I had saved the Accounts Board.
I hope this makes sense, I do not want to alter the order of items in the Sales Overview Board, I only want to make the Accounts column dropdown to be in alphabetical order like it is on the Accounts Board.
Thanks in advance for any assistance you can give!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @KMcG,

Thanks for following up. Just to clarify, are you referring to the connect boards column that isn’t displaying the items in the correct order (in the column) as they display in the native board?

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT ,

We do not want to sort our board by the connect column (it is already sorted by a deadline date).
The information we need to have sorted is the info in the actual dropdown menu. I have included a screenshot to show what I am seeing. In the screenshot you can see the accounts in the dropdown are not in alphabetical order (starts with C, then M, then Q, then B and so on - it appears to be the order each account was added). We would like the account dropdown to match the Account board’s sorting style (alphabetical).

image253×526 9.99 KB

Thanks in advance! Have a good day.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for clarifying this for me! After investigating internally, my understanding is that the order of those items (/accounts) in the column dropdown, should match the order the items appear in the native board (accounts board) - therefor if you have ordered them alphabetically this should display in the accounts column. As such, I am afraid this sounds like a potential bug, and recommend you reach out to our support team via support@monday.com so our technical team can investigate this matter for you I apologise for the double work here!

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks, @BiancaT - I appreciate your assistance in this.
I have sent an email to your support team, as you suggested.
Have a great week!

posts.author: amiraenergyedc (Amira R)
posts.cookedHtml: It would still be great to have the options create in a dropdown or status column to be ordered alphabetically! for example, in this status column it has things listed in the order they were added but can there be a button added maybe next to edit labels that says order A-Z or Z-A just for the status options so that when clicked into it would list the states in order. it shouldnt do anything for the board at large

posts.author: jessicaward (Jessica Ward)
posts.cookedHtml: We are currently trialling Monday. I’m in the process of migrating data from another platform in order to evaluate properly. I’ve just imported a list of contacts with dozens of different labels and I have to manually alphabetise them? I’m really baffled that Monday doesn’t have this basic functionality, but I’m even more disappointed to hear that it’s been FOUR YEARS since users first started taking the time to request it and Monday still hasn’t implemented.

posts.author: abacusblackburn (Abe Blackburn)
posts.cookedHtml: Bumping this…in 2025! Such a basic feature, we need to be able to sort the order of entries within a dropdown list.

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: another bump! PLEASE fix.

posts.author: Chana (Chana)
posts.cookedHtml: we need this too. This exists in the status and labels but not in dropdowns. seems like a basic feature!

posts.author: notahappymondayuser (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml: How has this been an issue for almost 6 years with no resolution? This is ridiculous.

posts.author: kcastaneda (Kacey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree! We are just starting to use dropdowns to replace a bunch of text columns but have a couple that have a bunch of option choices and I can’t get them into a order that makes sense!

I’ve attached a screenshot for reference showing a list of warranty years. I’ve been trying to get them to go from lowest to highest but every time I try to relabel them so that they’re in the correct order, they just load in a random order. Very frustrating!

Screen Shot 2025-11-17 at 11.05.06 AM241×431 12.1 KB
